title: Deliciously Roasted Ethiopian Lentils Misir Wot
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring roasted lentils in a spicy amazake sauce, this recipe is a crispy and nostalgic take on the classic misir wot. It is a buddhist-temple-friendly dish that incorporates bhuna spices for added depth.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the amazake, bhuna spice blend, berbere spice, salt, and black pepper.
Add the rinsed lentils to the bowl and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Spread the lentil mixture on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast the lentils in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the lentils are roasting, heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Once the lentils are done roasting, add them to the skillet with the onion mixture and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the misir wot for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
